title: quercus pentacycla
intro: Quercus pentacycla, also known as the five-bracted oak, is a deciduous tree species native to China. The medicinal properties of Quercus pentacycla lie in its bark, which is rich in flavonoids and phenolic acids, exhibiting anti-inflammatory and antioxidant activities. The health benefits of the plant include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve cardiovascular health, and exhibit antimicrobial properties. Quercus pentacycla is typically prepared as a decoction or an infusion, and its herbal preparations are used in traditional Chinese medicine to treat various ailments, including fever and respiratory issues.
